red_baseline.reason: T013 owns the failure-first shared lifecycle, reuse/concurrency, ownership, low-risk, partial restart, and forbidden-input contract; T014 owns implementation and CLI migration.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED: all three no-effect refusal contracts pass; five lifecycle/reuse/concurrency/partial/indeterminate cases fail only because createTaskHandlers does not yet expose T014 mutation handlers.

validation.summary: All three T013 contract files parse successfully.
3/8 refusal contracts pass; the remaining five failures are missing T014 task mutation handlers and therefore produce zero provider calls/spawn receipts.
Syntax check passed for 80 JavaScript files.

review.evidence: The RED contract freezes low-only dynamic risk, verified ownership, forbidden raw/force/approval inputs, one-spawn sequential/concurrent convergence, honest reusedExistingRun/effects semantics, one-identity partial restart, and indeterminate foreground reconciliation without broadening public inputs or retrying work.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Every positive and negative lifecycle case enters through Application Kernel with an injected existing lifecycle authority; tests define no parallel state backend.
Configured medium/high/destructive task risk refuses before journal, lock, handler, spawn, or signal; only low-risk positive fixtures may reach handlers.
Repeated start is resource-idempotent rather than replay-based, and indeterminate task.run exposes only operation.reconcile for the original operation ID.
The contract requires lifecycle mutations to execute only as Kernel handlers after shared project/risk/ownership/compatibility policy.
Sequential and synchronized concurrent starts require one spawn receipt, one run ID, reusedExistingRun=true, and changed=false on later results.
Unknown and external ownership for start/stop/restart is inspect-only with zero handler, spawn, and signal calls.
Tests source current task risk from Kernel taskResolver and prove host/model annotations cannot override it.
command/cwd/env/force/forceOwned/yes/confirmation/approval/shell/path/remote/url and policy override fields all fail closed as input_invalid before effects.
The same Kernel risk gate covers start/stop/restart/run and refuses every medium-plus configured task.
Permissive trustedContext approval/force/annotations cannot elevate input, risk, or ownership decisions.
Managed-start results are durable run identities and the lifecycle handler contract contains no adapter/MCP close or teardown coupling.
Unknown foreground completion is indeterminate with changed=null and exactly one operation.reconcile action referencing the original operation ID; the run provider is called once.
Only explicit task.start reaches the start mutation port; adoption remains absent from task mutation handlers and no discovery route is chained in these contracts.
